mapping.a_element: constant LGT transfer rate driving a Poisson process of events
mapping.b_element: per-round survival alpha and coverage c parameters of the urn

mapping.a_element: closed-form gene tree / site pattern probabilities as functions of the rate
mapping.b_element: closed-form containment and discovery curve U(s,T) as functions of (alpha, c)

mapping.a_element: identifiability question: can LGT and ILS be distinguished from the probabilities
mapping.b_element: two projections agree under a homogeneous urn, disagreement signaling a second component

mapping.a_element: two competing discordance processes (LGT vs ILS)
mapping.b_element: single vs two-component (persistent core kappa + shell) urn

brief_justification: The correspondences are only at the generic level of probabilistic modeling and parameter identifiability from closed-form probabilities; the actual generative machinery differs — A is a continuous-time Poisson point process of transfer events overlaid on a multispecies-coalescent genealogy, whereas B is a discrete-round urn sampling model of population survival and replacement, so no role-for-role mapping of the same governing process holds.
